profiles.scope: modify-existing only: animation leaves that already carry an explicit override can be previewed and exactly reverted; leaves in inherited state cannot (no revert-to-inherit exists)
profiles.runtime_command_strategy: hl.animation({ leaf = NAME, enabled = BOOL, speed = NUMBER, bezier = NAME }) via the eval runner
profiles.record_rendering_strategy: one Lua table per animation leaf with the four schema fields
profiles.original_capture_strategy: parse the leaf's overriden/enabled/speed/bezier/style from the read-only animations listing before any mutation
profiles.preview_value_strategy: minimal delta on speed for an already-overridden leaf
profiles.revert_strategy: reapply the captured explicit values and verify via readback; refuse leaves whose original state is inherited
profiles.verification_strategy: read-only animations listing before and after apply and after revert
profiles.ui_status: Supervised preview available for explicitly overridden animation records
profiles.next_proof_needed: env-gated modify-existing round trip on the explicitly overridden global node (zero residue)
profiles.evidence: live proof: the explicitly overridden global node round-tripped (speed 8.00 -> 8.5 -> 8.00) with readback-verified apply and exact restore; enabled is mandatory and revert-to-inherit does not exist, so inherited leaves are excluded from scope

profiles.scope: modify-existing only: bezier curves already present in the runtime can be redefined and exactly restored; creating new curves is excluded (no deletion mechanism)
profiles.runtime_command_strategy: hl.curve(NAME, { type = "bezier", points = { {X0, Y0}, {X1, Y1} } }) via the eval runner
profiles.record_rendering_strategy: curve table with type and control points matching the readback fields
profiles.original_capture_strategy: parse the curve's X0/Y0/X1/Y1 from the read-only animations listing before any mutation
profiles.preview_value_strategy: minimal control-point delta on an existing curve
profiles.revert_strategy: redefine the curve with the captured original points and verify via readback; refuse curve creation
profiles.verification_strategy: read-only animations bezier listing before and after apply and after revert
profiles.ui_status: Supervised preview available for existing bezier curve records
profiles.next_proof_needed: env-gated modify-existing round trip on the built-in default curve (zero residue)
profiles.evidence: live proof: the built-in default curve was redefined ({ type, points } table form), verified via bezier readback, and restored exactly with zero residue; hl.config bezier keys are silently ignored or rejected

profiles.original_capture_strategy: none: no gesture record readback mechanism was identified
profiles.verification_strategy: none identified: hyprctl exposes no gesture record listing
profiles.ui_status: Blocked: gesture records cannot be verified after a runtime change
profiles.blocked_reason: the runtime API exists (hl.gesture, schema requires a fingers field), but there is no readback mechanism to verify apply or revert, and no touch hardware is present to observe behavior
profiles.next_proof_needed: a gesture record readback mechanism plus touch hardware
profiles.evidence: live probe: hl.gesture exists (missing required field fingers); no readback found

profiles.original_capture_strategy: read-only monitor listing exists but is not sufficient recovery
profiles.revert_strategy: none: a failed monitor change can blank the screen, and revert must not require the user to see it
profiles.verification_strategy: hyprctl monitors readback exists but cannot confirm the user can still see the screen
profiles.ui_status: Blocked: display changes need a blind recovery system before any preview
profiles.blocked_reason: monitor records can disable or misconfigure displays; live preview requires a display dead-man recovery system that reverts without user sight, which does not exist
profiles.next_proof_needed: a display recovery system with blind auto-revert and its own gates
profiles.evidence: live probe: hl.monitor exists (output field required); risk model unchanged

profiles.revert_strategy: unproven: no verified unbind round trip, and a wrong bind can capture input
profiles.verification_strategy: hyprctl binds readback exists but does not remove the input risk
profiles.ui_status: Blocked: keybind records can capture or break input control
profiles.blocked_reason: bind records change input control; a wrong bind can swallow the keys needed to confirm or revert; requires a strong fallback model before any preview
profiles.next_proof_needed: a proven unbind round trip plus a secondary input fallback model
profiles.evidence: live probe: hl.bind exists (expects string arguments); binds readback exists via hyprctl binds

profiles.verification_strategy: hyprctl devices readback exists but device reconfiguration risk stands
profiles.ui_status: Blocked: device records reconfigure input hardware
profiles.blocked_reason: device records target specific input hardware; a wrong record can disable the device used to confirm or revert
profiles.next_proof_needed: per-device proof with a guaranteed unaffected secondary device
profiles.evidence: live probe: hl.device exists (name field required)

profiles.ui_status: Blocked: permission records are security policy
profiles.blocked_reason: permission records grant or deny capabilities to binaries; security policy is never live previewed
profiles.next_proof_needed: none planned: security policy stays config-write only
profiles.evidence: live probe: hl.permission exists (expects { binary, type, mode }); policy unchanged
